key_facts: Platform markets, also known as multi-sided markets, connect two or more distinct user groups whose interactions generate value through network effects.
A network effect occurs when the value of a product or service increases as more people use it.
Direct network effects arise within a single user group—as on a telephone network where each additional subscriber increases the network's value for all existing users.
Cross-side network effects, characteristic of platforms like Amazon Marketplace, Airbnb, and payment systems, occur when an increase in users on one side increases value for users on the other side.
Winner-take-all dynamics are common in platform markets because strong network effects create a self-reinforcing cycle that entrenches market leaders.
